source_paper_title: On Stability of the Scholtes Regularization for Mathematical Programs with Complementarity Constraints
context:
Page 17, Conclusion section (end of the paper)

In particular, the question on the homotopy equivalence of the corresponding lower level sets of MPCC and its Scholtes regularization can be addressed now.
title: Homotopy equivalence of MPCC and Scholtes-regularized lower level sets for small regularization parameter
background_latex:
Let \(f\in C^2(\mathbb{R}^n,\mathbb{R})\) and \(F_1,F_2\in C^2(\mathbb{R}^n,\mathbb{R}^\kappa)\). Define the MPCC feasible set
\[
M:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n\mid F_{1,j}(x)\,F_{2,j}(x)=0,\; F_{1,j}(x)\ge 0,\;F_{2,j}(x)\ge 0,\; j=1,\dots,\kappa\}.
\]
For \(t>0\), define the Scholtes regularization feasible set
\[
M_S(t):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n\mid F_{1,j}(x)\,F_{2,j}(x)\le t,\; F_{1,j}(x)\ge 0,\;F_{2,j}(x)\ge 0,\; j=1,\dots,\kappa\}.
\]
For any \(\alpha\in\mathbb{R}\), define the lower level (sublevel) sets
\[
L(\alpha):=\{x\in M\mid f(x)\le \alpha\},\qquad L_S(t,\alpha):=\{x\in M_S(t)\mid f(x)\le \alpha\}.
\]
Two topological spaces \(A\) and \(B\) are homotopy equivalent if there exist continuous maps \(\phi:A\to B\) and \(\psi:B\to A\) such that \(\psi\circ\phi\) is homotopic to \(\mathrm{id}_A\) and \(\phi\circ\psi\) is homotopic to \(\mathrm{id}_B\).
problem_definition_latex: Problem: Determine conditions under which there exists \(t_0>0\) such that for every \(t\in(0,t_0)\) and every \(\alpha\in\mathbb{R}\), the MPCC lower level set \(L(\alpha)\) and the Scholtes-regularized lower level set \(L_S(t,\alpha)\) are homotopy equivalent.
latex:
Let \(f\in C^2(\mathbb{R}^n,\mathbb{R})\) and \(F_1,F_2\in C^2(\mathbb{R}^n,\mathbb{R}^\kappa)\). Define the MPCC feasible set
\[
M:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n\mid F_{1,j}(x)\,F_{2,j}(x)=0,\; F_{1,j}(x)\ge 0,\;F_{2,j}(x)\ge 0,\; j=1,\dots,\kappa\}.
\]
For \(t>0\), define the Scholtes regularization feasible set
\[
M_S(t):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n\mid F_{1,j}(x)\,F_{2,j}(x)\le t,\; F_{1,j}(x)\ge 0,\;F_{2,j}(x)\ge 0,\; j=1,\dots,\kappa\}.
\]
For any \(\alpha\in\mathbb{R}\), define the lower level (sublevel) sets
\[
L(\alpha):=\{x\in M\mid f(x)\le \alpha\},\qquad L_S(t,\alpha):=\{x\in M_S(t)\mid f(x)\le \alpha\}.
\]
Two topological spaces \(A\) and \(B\) are homotopy equivalent if there exist continuous maps \(\phi:A\to B\) and \(\psi:B\to A\) such that \(\psi\circ\phi\) is homotopic to \(\mathrm{id}_A\) and \(\phi\circ\psi\) is homotopic to \(\mathrm{id}_B\).

Problem:
Problem: Determine conditions under which there exists \(t_0>0\) such that for every \(t\in(0,t_0)\) and every \(\alpha\in\mathbb{R}\), the MPCC lower level set \(L(\alpha)\) and the Scholtes-regularized lower level set \(L_S(t,\alpha)\) are homotopy equivalent.
